program.name: Transurban graduate program: Journey into the Future
program.duration_text: 18-month rotational program (three six-month rotations in a core discipline).
program.rotation_text: Three six-month rotations in a core discipline.

eligibility.graduation_window: Applicants are expected to intend to complete tertiary studies by February 2025, or to have completed in the last two years.
disciplines: Science (Engineering Science, Computer and Software Systems)
Technology (Information Technology, Business Information Systems, Computer Systems, Computer Science, Software Engineering or Software Development)
minimum_requirements: Applicants are expected to be completing (by February 2025) or have recently completed (in the last two years) a relevant bachelor’s degree with a credit average.

stages.name: Step 1: Apply online
stages.details: Apply online. The graduate program page states that applications for the 2025 graduate program are closed.

stages.name: Step 2: Psychometric testing
stages.details: Complete online assessments. These examine your abilities and preferences when working with others, across changing conditions, and with different types of information. Instructions and practice questions are provided in advance.

stages.name: Step 3: Video interview
stages.details: Complete a one-way video assessment where you record answers to set questions. This stage is used to assess your strengths, motivations, and alignment to Transurban’s core values.

stages.name: Steps 4 and 5: Presentation and interview
stages.details: Attend interactive, practical activities that may include a presentation and an interview. The presentation may assess how you work with others to solve a business problem, your follow-up process, and your reflections. The interview includes structured, behavioural questions aligned to the graduate role requirements, and you can ask questions about the program.

stages.name: Step 6: Offer and reference checks
stages.details: Transurban contacts your nominated referees to understand your strengths, past achievements, and ideal working environment.

stages.name: Step 7: Onboarding commences
stages.details: You are introduced to the 2024 graduates, your people leader, and your buddy before you start. You learn more about how Transurban operates and how you can grow and develop your career.

stages.name: Step 8: First day
stages.details: You are welcomed by your people leader and team, complete a facilities induction, and later in your first month complete a corporate induction. Your official graduate experience then commences.

tips: Follow Transurban on LinkedIn to stay up to date with Graduate and Student Community news.
Use the video interview stage to clearly explain your strengths, motivations, and how you align to Transurban’s core values.
Prepare to discuss how you work with others to solve business problems (for the presentation and interview stage).

exec_themes: Grow and improve a portfolio of toll roads through long-term concessions and major upgrades/expansions (build and operate).
Convert traffic growth and tolling outcomes into steady cash generation and security holder distributions.
Use customer-facing products and data (for example Linkt app and rewards) to strengthen customer value and support demand.
Work with governments and partners on transport policy and reforms that affect tolling and customer outcomes (example: NSW toll reform).
Improve safety, reliability, and network performance through operations and technology upgrades.
Decarbonisation and climate resilience planning (net zero pathway, renewables, lower-carbon operations and supply chain).
commercial_context.profit_engine: Transurban’s main profit engine is operating toll roads under long-term concession-style arrangements and turning road usage (traffic volumes) and toll revenue into cash flow that supports distributions to security holders.
commercial_context.headwinds: Transurban flags an “uncertain macroeconomic environment,” and its reporting shows ongoing exposure to financing-related risks (for example interest-rate and foreign-exchange risk management due to significant borrowings and international operations). In addition, tolling policy/regulatory reform can create uncertainty and may require changes to customer fees and processes (example: NSW toll reform discussions and commitments).
commercial_context.esg: Transurban’s ESG focus includes a net-zero greenhouse gas emissions target by 2050 across Scope 1, 2 and 3, with interim targets (including reducing absolute Scope 1 and 2 emissions 50% by 2030) and Scope 3 intensity targets that it says are aligned with SBTi methodology. It also describes actions like improving road efficiency, shifting its own operations away from fossil fuels, and working with supply chain partners on emissions reductions.
commercial_context.recent_pivot: Transurban has recently leaned further into customer/value features and engagement (for example, adding a personalised travel-time savings feature in the Linkt app and expanding/trialling Linkt Rewards), alongside active engagement in tolling reform in Sydney (including indicating willingness to remove NSW toll notice administration fees by mid-2026 and working toward a broader reform package in the first half of 2026).

notes: Program name, locations, duration, rotations, and listed disciplines/streams were taken directly from the Transurban Graduate Program page.
Eligibility and degree requirements were taken from the 'About you' section of the Transurban Graduate Program page.
No explicit work rights or visa/PR/citizenship requirement was stated on the page, so work_rights is null.

Graduate-specific recruitment stages (including psychometric testing, one-way video interview, and presentation/interview activities) were taken from the 'Next steps and process' section on the Transurban Graduate Program page.
The direct apply link provided points to the Transurban Careers 'Opportunities' page, which links out to Workday for job applications.

Some key strategy/risk detail typically found in investor presentations/transcripts could not be fully extracted here because the FY25 Corporate Report PDF could not be opened with the tool in this session (fetch error), so themes/headwinds are based on accessible excerpts and official web pages.

“Headwinds” is summarized at a high level from explicit references to macro uncertainty and documented financial risk exposures (e.g., FX/borrowings) rather than a complete principal-risks list from the report.
“Recent pivot” is framed from concrete, dated announcements and FY25 highlights (customer feature/rewards, NSW toll reform) rather than an explicit statement that management calls it a pivot.
